Name: Anthropic-Amazon New Carlisle
Notes: Anthropic-Amazon New Carlisle is part of the Anthropic-Amazon Project Rainier collaboration. It is similar to other Amazon locations, forgoing most external cooling infrastructure in favor of direct air based cooling.
Selected Sources:
- [Air Construction Permit application for the northern portion](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1BKiTFNUk2KDaUaiHG7fz0KlkNSBTHzbb/view?usp=sharing)
- [Backup diesel generator referenced in the permit](https://www.cat.com/en_US/products/new/power-systems/electric-power/diesel-generator-sets/117341.html)
- [Announcement of Project Rainier coming online](https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/aws/aws-project-rainier-ai-trainium-chips-compute-cluster)

Name: Meta Prometheus
Notes: Meta Prometheus is unusual for its ad hoc construction, spanning the full length of New Albany Business Park. A combination of weatherproof tents, colocation facilities and Meta’s traditional data center buildings, this data center shows Meta’s pivot towards AI. Reflecting that patchwork, our analysis uses a combination of land use maps, natural gas turbine permitting, and satellite/aerial imagery of cooling equipment to estimate compute capacity.
Selected Sources:
- [Meta blog post with details on Prometheus](https://engineering.fb.com/2025/09/29/data-infrastructure/metas-infrastructure-evolution-and-the-advent-of-ai/)
- [New Albany Business Park land use map](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1Wb_BBVOnhu5UCQ8Q5d6iRIG9K_vigD8A/view?usp=sharing)
- [Letter on Socrates South Power Generation Project](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1OMbFbdNGPmfSkwVWqOAK-5pOufiKQBu1/view?usp=sharing)

Name: Microsoft Fairwater Wisconsin
Notes: Microsoft Fairwater Wisconsin is set to be the tech giant’s most powerful data center yet. The buildings have a two-story structure, with GPU racks networked vertically as well as horizontally. Each of these GPU buildings is paired with a one-story building dedicated to data storage and processing using CPUs. We estimated compute capacity based on disclosures from Microsoft, and permit applications showing the layout of server racks. Data on Building 3 and 4 is more uncertain, as it is based on the relative sizing of the buildings and mechanical areas in architectural drawings (see this [calculation](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/10bfo173cjvehaMqextiRGc95lBdPmtZbLDyNvbEN0N0/edit?gid=0#gid=0&range=B40)).
Selected Sources:
- [Microsoft blog with engineering details](https://blogs.microsoft.com/blog/2025/09/18/inside-the-worlds-most-powerful-ai-datacenter/)
- [Microsoft blog with more general information](https://blogs.microsoft.com/on-the-issues/2025/09/18/made-in-wisconsin-the-worlds-most-powerful-ai-datacenter/)
- [Construction Permit Application for building 1](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1azyXnreQBuYEqX2oaSUgePDGCMneez5Z/view?usp=sharing)
- [Construction Permit Application for building 3 and 4 ](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1UXsNFoXCbstOK8upixx060-LFj8beHqp/view?usp=sharing)

Name: OpenAI Stargate Abilene
Notes: The Stargate data center campus in Abilene Texas is expected to host OpenAI's largest compute cluster from the second half of 2025 through 2026. This is the first of several sites in the Stargate project, spanning the US and the globe. We have a variety of sources to estimate the capacity of this data center, from company announcements, to permit documents, to satellite imagery of air-cooled chillers.
Selected Sources:
- [Crusoe 2024 Impact Report, with details on Abilene](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1oHWA9vmpvFOh-LvDy5fgq_wq5ITuxd1r/view?usp=sharing)
- [Air Permit Application for buildings 1-2, featuring blueprints on p.157](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1FgfX6hqojlBtqNXJHjzJmnwWAMx4_M0b/view?usp=sharing)
- [Mortensen’s profile of the project, with details of substations](https://www.mortenson.com/projects/abilene-data-center-development)

Name: Microsoft Fairwater Atlanta
Notes: Microsoft Fairwater Atlanta is a QTS-built data center with Microsoft as a tenant, possibly for the purpose of providing compute to OpenAI. We estimate compute for it primarily based on satellite and aerial imagery of the chillers.
Selected Sources:
- [2023 Air Permit application](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1p7aY9syz319IcEuyukgIIvzmhTNKRS6p/view?usp=sharing) 
- [YouTube video with aerial views](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=44eWesI-174) 
- [Semianalysis presentation](https://youtu.be/hobvps-H38o?si=wsAcRtDFG4Df4_7z&t=901) 
- [Bloomberg article](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/features/2024-12-08/georgia-s-blackstone-backed-qts-data-center-hits-resistance-over-ai-power-needs)

Name: xAI Colossus 2
Notes: xAI Colossus 2 is the follow-up to Colossus 1. Like its predecessor, it is located in Memphis and was converted from an existing building. To start powering the data center, xAI made the unusual choice to install natural gas turbines across the border in Mississippi, possibly to get faster approval for their operation. We use a combination of satellite/aerial images and announcements to estimate the timeline and compute capacity of the data center.
Selected Sources:
- [WSJ profile of the Colossus data centers](https://www.wsj.com/tech/elon-musk-xai-memphis-tennessee-power-dec4c70d) 
- [Final GPU count reported by Elon Musk](https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1947701807389515912)
- [MLGW update mentioning power requests for Colossus 2](https://www.mlgw.com/images/content/files/pdf/new/5-5-25%20xAI%20Update.pdf#page=4)

Name: xAI Colossus 1
Notes:
xAI Colossus 1 was converted from a factory into a data center in 2024. It is unusual among large scale AI data centers in the mixture of different types of cooling and power infrastructure.

Initially, Colossus 1 was powered by portable gas turbines and a small pre-existing substation. This infrastructure has since been joined by two additional substations providing power from the local grid, with batteries providing backup power. Cooling for the building is accomplished with both air-cooled chillers and cooling towers.
Selected Sources:
- [May 2025 Electricity Provider Disclosure](https://drive.google.com/file/d/17WAFThn95X85ebzA_e19R1zYycwFzAl2/view?usp=sharing)
- [July 2025 Permit for Gas Turbines](https://www.scribd.com/document/883601734/01156-01PC-070225-Permit)
- [Southern Environmental Law Center Gas Permit Appeal, with additional satellite images](https://cdn.arstechnica.net/wp-content/uploads/2025/07/NAACP-and-YGGs-xAI-Air-Permit-Appeal-7-15-2025.pdf)
- [Disclosure on X (formerly Twitter) by Elon Musk of the breakdown of GPUs](https://archive.is/oHQSA)

Name: Amazon Madison Mega Site
Notes: Amazon's Madison Mega Site is similar to other Amazon locations, forgoing most external cooling infrastructure in favor of direct air based cooling. We believe it is plausibly part of the Anthropic-Amazon Project Rainier collaboration, based on the New York Times reporting that there’s at least one such data center in Mississippi, though Amazon's data center in Ridgeland is also there. Analysts at SemiAnalysis have indicated this data center is an Anthropic-Amazon collaboration.
Selected Sources:
- [Air Construction Permit](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1lhSF8TBA7fNWsN4Onj-nfL7X_-BN573r/view?usp=sharing)
- [Backup diesel generator referenced in the permit](https://www.cat.com/en_US/products/new/power-systems/electric-power/diesel-generator-sets/117341.html)
- [New York Times article on Project Rainier](https://www.nytimes.com/2025/06/24/technology/amazon-ai-data-centers.html)
- [SemiAnalysis article with imagery of Canton they have labeled as being an Amazon-Anthropic collaboration](https://newsletter.semianalysis.com/p/amazons-ai-resurgence-aws-anthropics-multi-gigawatt-trainium-expansion)
Address: Amazon Data Services Inc, Madison Mega Site Nissan Parkway and Highway 22 Canton, Mississippi Madison County

Name: Google New Albany
Notes: Google’s New Albany data center is one of several data centers in the New Albany Business Park. We only include the buildings added or modified since 2024, when AI-specific capacity is more likely. We estimate the compute capacity primarily based on satellite and aerial imagery of the cooling towers, which are similar to towers documented at Google’s Omaha site.
Selected Sources:
- [Draft basis of air permit for Google Omaha, a similar data center](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1KN7o-wQOyt1UEud8wpM8PymXJF479r0n/view?usp=sharing)
- Google’s [2025 Environmental Report](https://sustainability.google/reports/google-2025-environmental-report/)
Address: 1101 Beech Rd SW, New Albany, OH 43054

Name: Microsoft Goodyear
Notes:
The Microsoft Goodyear campus spans six years of construction and operations. Most of its compute capacity comes from Building 3 and 4, which we believe house NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 servers based on the density of cooling equipment. SemiAnalysis [reported](https://newsletter.semianalysis.com/p/microsofts-ai-strategy-deconstructed) that this data center was intended for OpenAI use.

We estimate power capacity for Building 3 and 4 based on our air-cooled chiller model, and then calculate a power capacity for Building 2-3 based on the relative proportion of backup diesel generators. Compute capacity is based on the power capacity and the [availability](https://epoch.ai/data/ai-chip-sales) of different NVIDIA chips over time.
Selected Sources:
- [Maricopa County map](https://gis.maricopa.gov/aqd/recordsviewer/#) showing PHX-10 and PHX-11 buildings owned by Microsoft
- [Dust control permit](https://edms.maricopa.gov/aq/api/Document/AQizTh7Ek3Odb1UYnAFQc%C3%81Z0k59aCsu4%C3%890gw6xcPqnhTfYtG7scD%C3%89BewWyX4qdzXFk%C3%81xL9%C3%89VthN71ODAxZuKKeU%3D/) for project titled "MICROSOFT CORPORATION PHX10-11 DATA CENTER OFFSITE IMPROVEMENTS"
- [SemiAnalysis article](https://newsletter.semianalysis.com/p/microsofts-ai-strategy-deconstructed) that includes similar estimates and associates the data center with OpenAI

Name: Meta Temple
Notes: Meta Temple is notable for a mid-flight change in construction. A traditional H-shaped Meta data center was partially built on the site by 2023, only to be demolished in favor of Meta's new AI-optimized data center design. The power capacity of the new design was reported by construction company JE Dunn, and is backed up by our cooling model. Later, a third data center building was added to the plan. Compute capacity assumes that NVIDIA GB300 NVL72 servers are used, as these are the most popular AI chips at the time.
Selected Sources:
- [JE Dunn project page](https://jedunn.com/projects/meta-temple-data-center/) mentioning a 152 MW power capacity
- [TDLR filing](https://www.tdlr.texas.gov/TABS/Search/Project/TABS2022017728) reporting the start and end date, square footage of Building 1 and 2
- [TDLR filing](https://www.tdlr.texas.gov/TABS/Search/Project/TABS2026008019) for Building 3

Name: Alibaba Zhangbei
Notes: Alibaba Zhangbei spans three adjacent sites, built from 2016 to 2025. It is part of China's "Eastern Data Western Compute" (东数西算) project launched in 2021. Zhangbei county is favorable for this project due to its abundant wind and solar power. We only track the power capacity added here since 2024, which is higher-density and more likely for AI workloads. The power capacity is based on the air-cooled chillers seen in satellite images. Compute capacity is mostly calculated from power, based on the estimated energy efficiency of leading hardware available in China.
Selected Sources:
- [Sinocities blog on Eastern Data Western Compute sites](https://sinocities.substack.com/p/how-is-chinas-eastern-data-western)
- [News on the Chahar site, with an aerial photo](https://web.archive.org/web/20250712063836/https://www.cscec.com/xwzx_new/zqydt_new/202504/3867074.html)
- [Hebei government page with 3D renderings of the Miaotan North and Chahar sites](https://web.archive.org/web/20251205164722/https://gxt.hebei.gov.cn/main/portal/cylzsDetail?id=9c133225-aa96-4eb7-9906-00acc34ddbff)
- [Announcement of the Hebei Zhangbei Intelligent Computing Center](https://web.archive.org/web/20241217034700/https://zhidx.com/p/343866.html), part of this data center
Address: Alibaba Zhangbei Yun Calculation Data Center, Zhangbei County, Zhangjiakou, Hebei, China, 076471

Name: Google Council Bluffs (East)
Notes: Google Council Bluffs dates back to 2014, but our analysis focuses on the buildings added to the east since 2024, which are likely to have most of the AI compute. These buildings are similar to other Google data centers like New Albany and Omaha. We estimate the power capacity based on our cooling tower model. The compute capacity is based on this power capacity, and the energy efficiency of Google TPU chips that are available when each building becomes operational.
Selected Sources:
- [Construction permit application form with site address](https://programs.iowadnr.gov/easyair/Public/EnSuite/Shared/Pages/Util/ServerRptViewer.aspx?sr=/IDNR/Reports/EASY/RPT_Construction_Application_A_GEN&SUBMISSION_RID=67330&SUB_FORM_RID=148293)
- [Construction permit application form with backup generator data](https://programs.iowadnr.gov/easyair/Public/EnSuite/Shared/Pages/Util/ServerRptViewer.aspx?sr=/IDNR/Reports/EASY/RPT_Construction_Application_E_EU&SUBMISSION_RID=67330&SUB_FORM_RID=148297) (believed to be for Building 3)

Name: Fluidstack Lake Mariner
Notes: Built on the shores of Lake Ontario at a former coal plant, the Terawulf-owned Lake Mariner campus serves a mix of crypto-mining and AI computing applications. We only track the AI buildings here. Buildings 1-2 (70 MW) are leased to G42 subsidiary Core42, while Buildings 3-5 (440 MW) are leased to Fluidstack. Our sources point to Fluidstack's buildings serving Google TPUs for Anthropic. We named this site after Fluidstack because it has leased the vast majority of the AI capacity.
Selected Sources:
- [Terawulf press release with details of Core42 lease for Buildings 1-2](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/99/terawulf-to-deliver-over-70-mw-of-data-center)
- [Terawulf press release with details and timeline for Buildings 1-5](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/132/terawulf-reports-fourth-quarter-and-full-year-2025-results)
- [DCD article suggesting that Fluidstack will deploy Google TPUs at Lake Mariner](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/google-offers-its-tpus-to-ai-cloud-providers-report/)
- [Announcement of Anthropic-Fluidstack partnership, including data center(s) in New York](https://www.anthropic.com/news/anthropic-invests-50-billion-in-american-ai-infrastructure)

Name: Google Cedar Rapids
Notes: Google's Cedar Rapids data center is similar to earlier Google data centers like New Albany and Omaha. Curiously, permitting suggests it will use more than twice as many cooling towers per building as those data centers, but most of the evidence suggests that the power capacity per building is largely unchanged.
Selected Sources:
- [MISO new load announcements](https://cdn.misoenergy.org/NEW%20LOAD%20ANNOUNCEMENTS%20IN%20MISO%20REGIONS%2012062024684954.pdf), featuring 600 MW for Google in Cedar Rapids
- [Permit application](https://www.govonlinesaas.com/LCPH/EasyAir/Public/EnSuite/Shared/Pages/util/StreamDoc.ashx?id=260&type=attachment) detailing the number of buildings, cooling towers, and backup generators.
Address: 5800 EDGEWOOD RD SW CEDAR RAPIDS, IA 52404

Name: Google Omaha
Notes: Google’s Omaha data center is their second in Nebraska, following a site in Papillion. We estimate its compute capacity primarily based on satellite and aerial imagery of the cooling towers, which are documented in a draft air permit.
Selected Sources:
- [Draft basis of air permit with details of cooling towers](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1KN7o-wQOyt1UEud8wpM8PymXJF479r0n/view?usp=sharing)
- Google’s [2025 Environmental Report](https://sustainability.google/reports/google-2025-environmental-report/)

Name: Vantage TX1
Notes: Vantage’s TX1 campus is one of two known campuses in the San Antonio area. It is comprised of two buildings: Building TX11, which has already finished construction and is operational, and Building TX12, which has just started construction. Power capacity was determined based on capacity reported in construction permitting and supported by estimation from satellite images. Compute capacity was estimated assuming B200s will be used in both. Based on air quality permits for the site, a third building (TX13) has been planned as well, though there are no signs of construction.
Selected Sources:
- [Construction Permitting for TX11](https://www.tdlr.texas.gov/TABS/Search/Project/TABS2024007561)
- [Construction Permitting for TX12](https://www.tdlr.texas.gov/TABS/Search/Project/TABS2026001234)
- [Air quality permitting that references TX13](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1rbF-RT5Sq-iZ26hK5cKzbM2K4Vq2vObY/view?usp=sharing)

Name: Google Pryor (North)
Notes: Dating back to 2007, Google Pryor serves a variety of uses, from [Google Workspace](https://support.google.com/a/answer/13190816?hl=en) to [machine learning](https://techcrunch.com/2022/05/11/google-launches-a-9-exaflop-cluster-of-cloud-tpu-v4-pods-into-public-preview/). Our analysis only considers those buildings completed since 2024, which are more likely to house modern AI compute. We estimate power capacity using our model of the cooling towers seen in satellite imagery. The compute capacity is derived from the power capacity, based on the energy efficiency of the best available Google TPU chips.
Selected Sources:
- [Google announces expansion of Pryor facility](https://blog.google/inside-google/company-announcements/google-american-innovation-oklahoma/)
- [Google 2025 Environmental Report](https://sustainability.google/reports/google-2025-environmental-report/) with PUE and water use on pp.109-110
- [News of a 9 exaflop TPUv4 cluster in the Pryor data center, launched in 2022](https://techcrunch.com/2022/05/11/google-launches-a-9-exaflop-cluster-of-cloud-tpu-v4-pods-into-public-preview/)

Name: Meta Hyperion
Notes: Meta Hyperion is built on an enormous tract of land, more than three times the size of New York City’s Central Park. It is set to host Meta’s next flagship AI cluster after Prometheus. We estimate its compute capacity primarily based on an application by Entergy for three 752 MW gas turbines linked to the site, as well as plans for a new 1.8 GW substation by the grid operator MISO.
Selected Sources:
- [Data center profile by Meta](https://datacenters.atmeta.com/richland-parish-data-center/)
- [Entergy's application for generation and transmission resources](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1FLY13apaHy4hI8RNVk9n_nlJpwFHtBrB/view?usp=sharing)
- [MISO update with 1.8 GW substation plan](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1D4eUHwEuirNi0g3IU_xGOti3PcC8fcXi/view?usp=sharing)

Name: Goodnight
Notes:
Crusoe's Goodnight data center is notable for a major mid-flight change in design. It was originally designed in 2024 for seven X-shaped buildings that closely mirror the first eight buildings of the OpenAI Stargate Abilene data center (also built by Crusoe). This was revised in 2025 to 6 buildings, with Buildings 3-6 using a simple rectangle shape.

Given the structural similarities to Abilene, it is plausible it is equipped with NVIDIA chips. Google is a potential user, based on reporting that Google is investing in a data center in Armstrong County.

Goodnight is a location near the data center, and it is used for naming in the tax abatement agreements we have inspected. However, the data center is actually located in the town of Claude, Texas.
Selected Sources:
- [Amended tax abatement agreement, with updated blueprints on p.46](https://www.co.armstrong.tx.us/upload/page/5361/Amended_Restated_Consolidated%20Second%20Property%20Tax%20Abatement%20Agreement%20%20GNDC1_ArmstrongCounty.pdf)
- [Original tax abatement agreement, with original blueprints and specs on the last page](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1ky5HhuhU8_JADNB2GziWa4WDRbTU4qtO/view?usp=sharing)
- [TABS database, with "Llano" data centers in the city of Claude](https://www.tdlr.texas.gov/TABS/Search/)
- [Press release reports Google investing in a new data center in Armstrong County](https://www.reuters.com/business/google-invest-40-billion-new-data-centers-texas-bloomberg-news-reports-2025-11-14/)

Name: Amazon Ridgeland
Notes: Amazon Ridgeland is similar to other Amazon locations, forgoing most external cooling infrastructure in favor of direct air based cooling. We believe it is plausibly part of the Anthropic-Amazon Project Rainier collaboration, based on the New York Times reporting that there’s at least one such data center in Mississippi, though Amazon's data center in Canton is also there.
Selected Sources:
- [Air Construction Permit](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1dQ5Pxl7rQAHLvHoTYrpOX32WAolo46d7/view?usp=sharing)
- [Permit review document](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1myQ6BYqqzcfsWwv41tufHobvA8T3qo_d/view?usp=sharing)
- [Backup diesel generator referenced in the permit](https://www.cat.com/en_US/products/new/power-systems/electric-power/diesel-generator-sets/117341.html)
- [New York Times article on Project Rainier](https://www.nytimes.com/2025/06/24/technology/amazon-ai-data-centers.html)

Name: OpenAI Stargate UAE
Notes: The Stargate data center campus in Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates, is the first non-US Stargate cluster announced by OpenAI. We estimate power capacity based on company announcements and the scale of buildings in satellite imagery, and also use that imagery to estimate the construction timeline. Compute capacity is based on reported export agreements with the US, which suggest that G42 and/or Oracle can acquire 100,000 state-of-the-art NVIDIA chips each year.
Selected Sources:
- [News update with timelapse of construction](https://www.khaleejtimes.com/uae/stargate-uae-phase-1-in-two-years)
- [Announcement of the (separate) 100 MW Khazna Ajman data center](https://khaznadatacenters.com/press-release/khazna-announces-the-uaes-first-ai-optimized-data-center-and-the-largest-in-its-portfolio-powering-the-nations-drive-toward-ai-leadership/), which started earlier and has a similar design to the Stargate buildings.
- [News about the US approval of NVIDIA chips in the UAE](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2025-10-09/us-approves-several-billion-dollars-of-nvidia-nvda-ai-chip-sales-to-uae)
Address: Nexus L&T Project Office, Al Bihouth, Al Dhafrah, Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates

Name: Coreweave Helios
Notes: Helios is retrofitted from a former crypto-mining facility owned and operated by Galaxy. Now in the AI infrastructure business, Galaxy is expanding the site. Galaxy plans to [lease](https://investor.galaxy.com/news/news-details/2025/Galaxy-Announces-Third-Quarter-2025-Financial-Results/default.aspx) its approved 800 MW of capacity to AI neo-cloud Coreweave. An additional 2.7 GW of capacity is under study, but even if that happens, it is not planned to be operational before 2029. We report the power capacity based on Galaxy's reports, which roughly match permit documents and our cooling model. Compute is estimated based on power capacity and the server rack density reported by Galaxy.
Selected Sources:
- [Galaxy Q3 financial results with power capacity timeline](https://investor.galaxy.com/news/news-details/2025/Galaxy-Announces-Third-Quarter-2025-Financial-Results/default.aspx)
- [Permit review with details on backup generators and power capacity](https://records.tceq.texas.gov/cs/idcplg?IdcService=TCEQ_EXTERNAL_SEARCH_GET_FILE&dID=8711478&Rendition=Web#:~:text=Galaxy%20Helios%20I%20LLC%20owns,are%20the%20basis%20of%20the)
- [3D renderings of Helios Building 1 on Galaxy's website, showing generators and chillers](https://www.galaxy.com/data-centers)
- [TDLR record with Building 1 construction costs and end date](https://www.tdlr.texas.gov/TABS/Search/Project/TABS2025012175)

Name: QTS Cedar Rapids
Notes: QTS Cedar Rapids sits alongside a Google data center in Big Cedar Industrial Center. It is divided into two phases which are expected to  be completed in late 2026 and late 2027. We estimate its power capacity based on satellite images and 3D renderings of the air-cooled chillers lining each building. The compute capacity is based on this power capacity and the assumption that the best available NVIDIA AI servers will be used for each phase. The estimated power for phase 1 is consistent with a 616 MW load request for a QTS shell company in Cedar Rapids. However, the total capacity across both phases is more than double that, suggesting that QTS will need to acquire more power by 2027 to fulfil their plan.
Selected Sources:
- [MISO new load announcements](https://cdn.misoenergy.org/NEW%20LOAD%20ANNOUNCEMENTS%20IN%20MISO%20REGIONS%2012062024684954.pdf), including 616 MW in Cedar Rapids for a "Shell Corp" called SNA LLC.
- [Article by The Gazette](https://www.thegazette.com/business/fast-start-to-cedar-rapids-data-center-projects/) featuring a rendering of Building 2 and 3.
- [Article by Corridor Business Journal ](https://corridorbusiness.com/scope-of-qts-data-project-in-cedar-rapids-continuing-to-grow/)featuring a rendering of Buildings 1-3.
- [Post by GLD Commercial ](https://www.gldcommercial.com/google-and-qts-data/)featuring a map with land assigned to QTS in Big Cedar Industrial Center.

Name: OpenAI Stargate Shackelford
Notes:
Stargate Shackelford is the second US site in the Stargate project led by OpenAI, Oracle and Softbank. Just 20km from the original Abilene site, this site is developed by data center colocation company Vantage, who refers to it as "Frontier". In September 2025, OpenAI announced a site in Shackelford County, Texas, and Vantage later confirmed their data center is part of Stargate.

The date that this campus reaches full capacity is speculative. However, Vantage states the planned capacity on their website, along with the delivery date of the first building in late 2026. We estimate compute capacity based on the best NVIDIA AI servers expected to be available as each building comes online.
Selected Sources:
- [Vantage data center page with power capacity, number of buildings, and first building timeline](https://vantage-dc.com/data-center-locations/north-america/shackelford-county-tx)
- [OpenAI Stargate expansion announcement mentioning Shackelford site](https://openai.com/index/five-new-stargate-sites/)
- [Vantage LinkedIn post connecting the data center to OpenAI Stargate](https://web.archive.org/web/20260226203340/https://www.linkedin.com/posts/vantage-data-centers_ai-digitalinfrastructure-stargate-activity-7405254667062124544-FOcd/?rcm=ACoAAAmuuF4BvsgagH4Z8-Z8-0LaBPPY6467cAs)
Address: 175 Private Road 1604, Abilene, TX 79601, Shackelford County

Name: Crusoe Abilene Expansion
Notes: Built by Crusoe and originally planned as an expansion to Stargate Abilene for OpenAI and Oracle, this site reportedly now has interest from NVIDIA and Meta but was ultimately acquired by Microsoft.
